grassTypes.name: Bermuda Grass
grassTypes.description: A fast-growing warm-season grass common in the southern United States. Very durable, thrives in full sun, and spreads aggressively.

grassTypes.name: Tall Fescue
grassTypes.description: A cool-season grass widely used in North Carolina. Deep roots make it drought tolerant. Grows in sun or partial shade.

grassTypes.name: Kentucky Bluegrass
grassTypes.description: A dense, dark-green cool-season grass. Spreads through underground rhizomes and creates a lush lawn.

grassTypes.name: Perennial Ryegrass
grassTypes.description: A quick-germinating grass often used for overseeding. Bright green color and fast establishment.

grassTypes.name: Zoysia Grass
grassTypes.description: A dense, carpet-like warm-season grass. Slow-growing but extremely heat tolerant and low maintenance.

grassTypes.name: Centipede Grass
grassTypes.description: A low-maintenance warm-season grass popular in the Southeast. Slow-growing and prefers acidic soil.

grassTypes.name: Bahia Grass
grassTypes.description: A tough, drought-resistant grass commonly used in rural or sandy areas. Low maintenance and durable.

grassTypes.name: St. Augustine Grass
grassTypes.description: A thick-bladed warm-season grass that thrives in shade. Usually sold as sod or plugs rather than seed.

grassTypes.name: Artificial Turf
grassTypes.description: Synthetic grass used for low-maintenance lawns. No watering, mowing, or growth required.
